title: TLS timeout leads to nil pointer error during image pull
body:
```
time="2018-10-05T03:25:04Z" level=debug msg="parsed reference into \"[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.skip_mount_home=false,overlay.mountopt=nodev,overlay.override_kernel_check=false]docker.io/centos/ruby-22-centos7:latest\""
Registry for "centos/ruby-22-centos7" is not present in the registries configuration, assuming it is secure.
time="2018-10-05T03:25:04Z" level=debug msg="parsed reference into \"[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.skip_mount_home=false,overlay.mountopt=nodev,overlay.override_kernel_check=false]docker.io/centos/ruby-22-centos7:latest\""
time="2018-10-05T03:25:04Z" level=debug msg="copying \"docker://centos/ruby-22-centos7\" to \"docker.io/centos/ruby-22-centos7:latest\""
time="2018-10-05T03:25:04Z" level=debug msg="Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration"
time="2018-10-05T03:25:04Z" level=debug msg=" No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/centos/ruby-22-centos7:latest"
time="2018-10-05T03:25:04Z" level=debug msg="Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/docker.io"
time="2018-10-05T03:25:04Z" level=debug msg="GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/"
time="2018-10-05T03:25:16Z" level=debug msg="Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ err Get https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/: net/http: TLS handshake timeout (&url.Error{Op:\"Get\", URL:\"https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/\", Err:http.tlsHandshakeTimeoutError{}})"
time="2018-10-05T03:25:16Z" level=debug msg="GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v1/_ping"
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x18 pc=0x12ec9d5]

goroutine 1 [running]:

	/go/src/github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/image/docker/docker_image_src.go:81 +0x57
github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/image/image.(*UnparsedImage).Manifest(0xc420092cc0, 0x19c7740, 0xc4200b8018, 0x1775e80, 0xc420964a18, 0x413658, 0x60, 0x16fab00, 0x1775e01, 0xc420092cc0)
	/go/src/github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/image/image/unparsed.go:46 +0xd7
github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/image/copy.isMultiImage(0x19c7740, 0xc4200b8018, 0x19b8b80, 0xc420092cc0, 0xc420964c28, 0xc420765dc0, 0x0)
	/go/src/github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/image/copy/manifest.go:116 +0x49
github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/image/copy.Image(0x19c7740, 0xc4200b8018, 0xc420729ae0, 0x19d8080, 0xc420092ba0, 0x19d83e0, 0xc4203166a0, 0xc420b1ee08, 0x0, 0x0)
	/go/src/github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/image/copy/copy.go:152 +0x340
github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah.pullImage(0x19c7740, 0xc4200b8018, 0x19f0e60, 0xc42032f0e0, 0xc420457a60, 0x1f, 0x0, 0x0, 0x19a8880, 0xc4200bc010, ...)
	/go/src/github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/pull.go:193 +0x49c
github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah.Pull(0x19c7740, 0xc4200b8018, 0xc420457a60, 0x1f, 0x0, 0x0, 0x19a8880, 0xc4200bc010, 0x19f0e60, 0xc42032f0e0, ...)
	/go/src/github.com/openshift/builder/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/pull.go:141 +0x163
github.com/openshift/builder/pkg/build/builder.pullDaemonlessImage(0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, ...)
	/go/src/github.com/openshift/builder/pkg/build/builder/daemonless.go:88 +0x881
github.com/openshift/builder/pkg/build/builder.(*DaemonlessClient).PullImage(0xc4203d4160, 0xc420524ce5, 0x16, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, 0x0, ...)
	/go/src/github.com/openshift/builder/pkg/build/builder/daemonless.go:556 +0x107
```

This should be either retried or reported as a failure, not cause a nil pointer failure.

comments.body:
I can’t reproduce a _crash_, but I do see some weird behavior and the code is definitely doing something it shouldn’t (and using two debug lines when one would do).

https://github.com/containers/image/pull/517 will hopefully make this better.
This should be fixed by #1082, closing.
